qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SFTP root confinement is bypassable for out-of-root read and rename
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding reaches the same SFTP `sftpserver/helper.go` containment bug described by the advisory: `sanitizePath()` accepts sibling paths outside the configured root because it relies on a raw `strings.HasPrefix` check. The finding also notes an extra rename-target issue, but its outside-root SFTP read path is the advisory's root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Shared-link token bypasses authentication on unrelated web routes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the HTTP BasicAuth middleware and share-token scoping logic, not the advisory's SFTP `sanitizePath()` prefix-validation flaw or the SFTP filesystem sinks it reaches.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Trusted proxy headers bypass the IP whitelist
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns HTTP/WebDAV client-IP trust and whitelist enforcement, which is a different middleware control path from the advisory's SFTP root-prefix validation bug.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTTP upload and delete handlers escape the configured webroot
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an HTTP path-handling bug in upload and delete helpers under `httpserver/`, not the advisory's SFTP root-escape in `sftpserver/helper.go`.

selected_finding.title: SFTP root confinement is bypassable for out-of-root read and rename

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory's SFTP root-cause mechanism in `sftpserver/helper.go`, and the copied PoC produced end-to-end runtime evidence that an authenticated SFTP client escaped the configured root and accessed sibling filesystem paths outside the intended jail.
evidence: The advisory and the selected finding both describe the SFTP `sanitizePath()` prefix-only containment check in `sftpserver/helper.go` as the root cause for sibling-path escape outside the configured root.

Runtime evidence in `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_acb0f47f/.judge/poc_runs/sftp_root_confinement_bypass/run.log` shows `sftp> get /tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/root2/secret.txt /tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/out.txt` succeeded even though the configured root in the PoC was `/tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/root`.

The same run log shows `sftp> rename /tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/root/in.txt /tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/pwned.txt` followed by `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: SFTP read escaped the configured root and rename moved a file outside it`, proving unauthorized outside-root filesystem access caused by the vulnerable SFTP confinement logic.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The Dockerized PoC authenticated over SFTP, fetched `/tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/root2/secret.txt` from a sibling path outside the configured root, renamed `/tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/root/in.txt` to `/tmp/sftp-root-bypass-poc/pwned.txt`, and emitted the `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS` marker.
